name: Toolbar
summary: The Toolbar class contains methods to create and manipulate SketchUp toolbars in Ruby. Toolbars are collections of buttons that you can use to activate custom Tools or ruby scripts. Also see the Command object for details on creating “commands” which can be called from your toolbars.

methods.name: new
methods.summary: The new method creates a new Toolbar object.
methods.remarks: The new method creates a new Toolbar object.

params.name: toolbarname
params.doc: The name for the new toolbar.

methods.name: add_item
methods.summary: The add_item method is used to add an item to the toolbar.
methods.remarks: The add_item method is used to add an item to the toolbar.

params.name: command
params.doc: A Command object representing the command to add to the toolbar.

returns.doc: the toolbar where the command was just added

methods.name: add_separator
methods.summary: The add_separator method is used to add a line separator to the toolbar.
methods.remarks: The add_separator method is used to add a line separator to the toolbar.

returns.doc: the toolbar where the line separator was just added

methods.name: count
methods.summary: The #count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: The #count method is inherited from the Enumerable mix-in module.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the commands attached to a toolbar.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through all of the commands attached to a toolbar.

methods.name: get_last_state
methods.summary: The get_last_state method is used to determine if the toolbar was hidden or visible in the previous session of SketchUp.
methods.remarks: The get_last_state method is used to determine if the toolbar was hidden or visible in the previous session of SketchUp. Valid states are TB_VISIBLE (1) for visible, TB_HIDDEN (0) for hidden, TB_NEVER_SHOWN (-1) for before never shown.

returns.doc: the last state of the toolbar (see constants above)

methods.name: hide
methods.summary: The hide method is used to hide the toolbar on the user interface.
methods.remarks: The hide method is used to hide the toolbar on the user interface.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method returns the number of items in the toolbar.
methods.remarks: The #length method returns the number of items in the toolbar.

methods.name: name
methods.summary: The name method returns the name of the toolbar.
methods.remarks: The name method returns the name of the toolbar.

methods.name: restore
methods.summary: The restore method is used to reposition the toolbar to its previous location and show if not hidden.
methods.remarks: The restore method is used to reposition the toolbar to its previous location and show if not hidden.

methods.name: show
methods.summary: The show method is used to display the toolbar in the user interface.
methods.remarks: The show method is used to display the toolbar in the user interface.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias of #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias of #length.

methods.name: visible?
methods.summary: The visible? method is used to find out if a toolbar is currently visible.
methods.remarks: The visible? method is used to find out if a toolbar is currently visible.
